name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
قَدْ نَعْلَمُ إِنَّهُۥ لَيَحْزُنُكَ ٱلَّذِى يَقُولُونَ ۖ فَإِنَّهُمْ لَا يُكَذِّبُونَكَ وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ يَجْحَدُونَ (٣٣)
```

```english
We certainly know that what they say grieves you. Yet it is not you that they deny, but it is Allah’s signs that the wrongdoers impugn. (33)
```

```arabic
وَلَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِّن قَبْلِكَ فَصَبَرُوا۟ عَلَىٰ مَا كُذِّبُوا۟ وَأُوذُوا۟ حَتَّىٰٓ أَتَىٰهُمْ نَصْرُنَا ۚ وَلَا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِمَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَكَ مِن نَّبَإِى۟ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ (٣٤)
```

```english
Apostles were certainly denied before you, yet they patiently bore being denied and tormented until Our help came to them. Nothing can change the words of Allah, and there have certainly come to you some of the accounts of the apostles. (34)
```

```arabic
وَإِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ إِعْرَاضُهُمْ فَإِنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتَ أَن تَبْتَغِىَ نَفَقًا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ أَوْ سُلَّمًا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَتَأْتِيَهُم بِـَٔايَةٍ ۚ وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَجَمَعَهُمْ عَلَى ٱلْهُدَىٰ ۚ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْجَـٰهِلِينَ (٣٥)
```

```english
And should their aversion be hard on you, find, if you can, a tunnel into the ground, or a ladder into sky, that you may bring them a sign. Had Allah wished, He would have brought them together on guidance. So do not be one of the ignorant. (35)
```

```arabic
۞ إِنَّمَا يَسْتَجِيبُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَسْمَعُونَ ۘ وَٱلْمَوْتَىٰ يَبْعَثُهُمُ ٱللَّهُ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ (٣٦)
```

```english
Only those who listen will respond [to you]. As for the dead, Allah will resurrect them, then they will be brought back to Him. (36)
```

## (Commentary)

This is a consolation for the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) regarding the lapses of the polytheists (المشركين) in the matter of his mission, and a heartening of his soul with the promise of inevitable victory. It is also an explanation that the religious mission (الدعوة الدينية) is situated within the sphere of human choice (الاختيار الإنساني); thus, whoever wills may believe, and whoever wills may disbelieve. The decisive Divine Power and Will do not intervene in this to the extent of compelling them to accept, for had Allah willed, He would have gathered them all upon guidance (الهدى).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **قَدْ نَعْلَمُ إِنَّهُ لَيَحْزُنُكَ الَّذِي يَقُولُونَ** — **"We certainly know that what they say grieves you"** until the end of the verse. The particle **قَدْ** is a particle of realization (تحقيق) when used with the past tense, while with the present tense, it usually denotes paucity (التقليل), though it is sometimes used for realization as well, which is the intended meaning in this verse. The terms **حزنه كذا** and **أحزنه** (it grieved him) carry the same meaning, and the verse has been recited using both forms.

Regarding His saying: **فَإِنَّهُمْ لا يُكَذِّبُونَكَ** — **"for it is not you that they cry lies to,"** it has been recited with the doubled consonant (التشديد) from the form *taf‘īl* (meaning to call someone a liar), and with the lightened form (التخفيف) (meaning to find someone to be a liar). It appears that the particle *fā’* in His saying **فَإِنَّهُمْ** is for derivation (التفريع). The meaning is as if to say: We know that their speech grieves you, but it should not grieve you, for their denial does not actually return to you. You only call them toward Us, and your only role is the delivery of the message (الرسالة); rather, by doing so, they wrong Our signs and reject them.

Thus, what is in this verse, along with His saying at the end of the verses: **ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ** — **"then to Him they shall be returned,"** is similar in meaning to His saying, the Exalted: **وَمَنْ كَفَرَ فَلا يَحْزُنْكَ كُفْرُهُ إِلَيْنا مَرْجِعُهُمْ فَنُنَبِّئُهُمْ بِما عَمِلُوا إِنَّ اللهَ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ** — **"As for him who is faithless, let his faithlessness not grieve you. To Us will be their return, and We will inform them of what they have done. Indeed Allah knows best what is in the breasts"** (Luqman: 23), and His saying: **فَلا يَحْزُنْكَ قَوْلُهمْ إِنَّا نَعْلَمُ ما يُسِرُّونَ وَما يُعْلِنُونَ** — **"So let not their speech grieve you. Indeed We know what they hide and what they expose"** (Ya-Sin: 76), and other verses revealed to console him (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله). This applies to the recitation with the doubled consonant.

As for the recitation with the lightened form, the meaning is: Do not grieve, for they do not prevail over you by proving you a liar in what you call them to, nor do they invalidate your proof with any counter-argument; they only wrong the signs of Allah by rejecting them, and to Him is their return.

His saying: **وَلكِنَّ الظَّالِمِينَ بِآياتِ اللهِ يَجْحَدُونَ** — **"but the wrongdoers deny the signs of Allah."** The apparent context would suggest saying "but they," yet the shift to the explicit noun "the wrongdoers" (الظالمين) is to indicate that their rejection (الجحد) stems from their own wrongdoing (ظلم), not from incapacity or ignorance. It is nothing but insolence, resentment, and rebellion, and Allah will resurrect them, then to Him they shall return.

For this reason, a transition (الالتفات) occurred in the speech from the first person to the third person, as it says: **بِآياتِ اللهِ** — **"the signs of Allah,"** rather than "Our signs." This is to indicate that their action is a confrontation with the station of Divinity (الألوهية) and an attempt to exalt themselves over it—a station against which nothing can stand.

Other views have been mentioned in the interpretation of this verse:

First: The view of the majority, that the meaning is: They do not deem you a liar in their hearts out of conviction, but they manifest denial with their mouths out of stubbornness.

Second: That they do not cry lies to you, but rather they cry lies to Me, for your denial returns to Me and is not exclusive to you. This view is distinct from the one we previously mentioned, though close to it. Both of these views apply to the recitation with the doubled consonant.

Third: That they do not find you to be a liar. The Arabs say: "We fought them but did not find them cowardly" (ما أجبناهم), meaning we did not find them to be cowards. However, the correct view is what was mentioned first.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ فَصَبَرُوا عَلى ما كُذِّبُوا** — **"Apostles were certainly denied before you, yet they patiently endured being denied"** until the end of the verse. This is a guidance for him (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) to the path of the prophets who preceded him, which is the path of patience (الصبر) for the sake of Allah. He, the Exalted, has said: **أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ هَدَى اللهُ فَبِهُداهُمُ اقْتَدِهْ** — **"They are the ones whom Allah has guided. So follow their guidance"** (Al-An'am: 90).

His saying: **حَتَّى أَتاهُمْ نَصْرُنا** — **"until Our help came to them,"** is an explanation of the beautiful end-result of their patience and an allusion to the Divine promise of victory. In His saying: **وَلا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِماتِ اللهِ** — **"and there is no changer of Allah’s words,"** there is a reinforcement of the promise alluded to in the preceding speech and an affirmation of its inevitability. It refers to what He mentioned in His saying: **كَتَبَ اللهُ لَأَغْلِبَنَّ أَنَا وَرُسُلِي** — **"Allah has ordained: ‘I shall surely prevail, I and My apostles’"** (Al-Mujadila: 21), and His saying: **وَلَقَدْ سَبَقَتْ كَلِمَتُنا لِعِبادِنَا الْمُرْسَلِينَ ، إِنَّهُمْ لَهُمُ الْمَنْصُورُونَ** — **"Certainly Our decree has gone forth for Our servants, the apostles, that they shall indeed be the victorious ones"** (Al-Saffat: 171-172).

The occurrence of "changer" (المبدل) in His saying **وَلا مُبَدِّلَ لِكَلِماتِ اللهِ** within a negative context negates any supposed changer, whether from His side—the Exalted—by His will changing regarding a specific word such that He erases it after establishing it or annuls it after confirming it, or from the side of someone other than Him—the Exalted—by prevailing over Him and forcing Him contrary to what He willed, thereby changing what He had made firm or altering it in any way.

From this, it becomes clear that these "words" (الكلمات) which He, the Glorified, informed are not subject to change, are matters outside the Tablet of Erasure and Establishment (لوح المحو والإثبات). The "Word of Allah" (كلمة الله) and His "Speech" (قوله), as well as His "Promise" (وعده) in the terminology of the Quran, refer to the Final Decree (القضاء الحتم) in which there is no hope for alteration or change. He, the Exalted, said: **قالَ فَالْحَقُّ وَالْحَقَّ أَقُولُ** — **"He said, ‘This is the truth, and the truth I say’"** (Sad: 84), and He, the Exalted, said: **وَاللهُ يَقُولُ الْحَقَّ** — **"and Allah speaks the truth"** (Al-Ahzab: 4), and He, the Exalted, said: **أَلا إِنَّ وَعْدَ اللهِ حَقٌّ** — **"Look! Allah’s promise is indeed true"** (Yunus: 55), and He, the Exalted, said: **لا يُخْلِفُ اللهُ الْمِيعادَ** — **"Allah does not break His promise"** (Az-Zumar: 20). A comprehensive discussion on the meaning of the "Words of Allah" and their synonymous terms in the terminology of the Quran has already passed under the commentary of His saying: **مِنْهُمْ مَنْ كَلَّمَ اللهُ** — **"of them were those to whom Allah spoke"** (Al-Baqarah: 253).

His saying at the end of the verse: **وَلَقَدْ جاءَكَ مِنْ نَبَإِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ** — **"and there have already come to you some of the accounts of the apostles,"** is a confirmation and a citation of evidence for His saying: **وَلَقَدْ كُذِّبَتْ رُسُلٌ مِنْ قَبْلِكَ** — **"Apostles were certainly denied before you..."** etc. It can be inferred from this that this Surah was revealed after some of the Meccan Surahs that narrate the stories of the prophets, such as Surah al-Shu'ara, Maryam, and their like. These Surahs were certainly revealed after the likes of Surah al-Alaq and al-Muddaththir. Thus, Surah al-An'am falls into the third layer of Surahs revealed in Mecca before the Migration (الهجرة), and Allah knows best.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَإِنْ كانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ إِعْراضُهُمْ** — **"And if their aversion is hard on you"** until His saying **فَتَأْتِيَهُمْ بِآيَةٍ** — **"and bring them a sign."** Al-Raghib said: **النفق** (the tunnel) is a through-way or an underground passage. He said: **فَإِنِ اسْتَطَعْتَ أَنْ تَبْتَغِيَ نَفَقاً فِي الْأَرْضِ** — **"then if you can, seek a tunnel into the earth."** From this comes the *nāfiqā’* of the jerboa (one of its burrow holes). The jerboa is said to *nāfaqa* or *nafaqa* (entered its hole). From this also comes hypocrisy (النفاق), which is entering the Sharia through one door and exiting it through another; Allah pointed this out by saying: **إِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ هُمُ الْفاسِقُونَ** — **"Indeed the hypocrites are the transgressors"** (At-Tawbah: 67), meaning those who exit the Sharia. Allah made the hypocrites worse than the disbelievers, saying: **إِنَّ الْمُنافِقِينَ فِي الدَّرْكِ الْأَسْفَلِ مِنَ النَّارِ** — **"Indeed the hypocrites will be in the lowest reach of the Fire"** (An-Nisa: 145). The *nayfaq* of trousers is well-known. (End of quote).

He also said: **السلم** (the ladder) is that by which one reaches high places, hoping for safety (سلامة). Then it was used as a name for anything by which one reaches something lofty, like a "means" (السبب). The Exalted said: **أَمْ لَهُمْ سُلَّمٌ يَسْتَمِعُونَ فِيهِ** — **"Or do they have a ladder whereby they listen?"** (At-Tur: 38), and He said: **أَوْ سُلَّماً فِي السَّماءِ** — **"or a ladder into the sky."** A poet said: "Even if he reached the means of the sky with a ladder." (End of quote).

The apodosis (جواب الشرط) of the conditional sentence in the verse is omitted because it is known. The estimation, as has been said, is: "If you are able to seek such-and-less, then do so."

The intended meaning of "sign" (آية) in His saying **فَتَأْتِيَهُمْ بِآيَةٍ** is a sign that would compel them to believe. The address in His saying: **وَإِنْ كانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ إِعْراضُهُمْ** — **"And if their aversion is hard on you"** etc., was delivered to the Prophet (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) by way of the Quran, which is the greatest Divine sign indicating the truth of his mission. Its miraculous nature (إعجازه) is close to their understanding, as they are eloquent and rational people. The meaning is that their aversion should not be hard or difficult for you, for this world is the abode of choice (الاختيار), and the call to truth and its acceptance proceed according to the course of choice. You are not able to obtain a sign that would necessitate belief for them or compel them to it, for Allah, the Glorified, did not intend for them to believe except by their own choice. Thus, He did not create a sign that forces people into faith and obedience. Had Allah willed, all people would have believed, and these disbelievers would have joined the believers with you. So do not be distressed or grieved by their aversion, lest you be among those ignorant of Divine knowledge.

As for what some have conjectured—that the meaning is: "bring them a sign that is better than the sign with which We sent you, i.e., the Quran"—this does not fit the context of the verse, especially His saying **وَلَوْ شاءَ اللهُ لَجَمَعَهُمْ عَلَى الْهُدى** — **"Had Allah wished, He would have gathered them on guidance,"** for it clearly points to compulsion (الاضطرار).

From here it becomes clear that the intended "Will" (المشية) is that Allah would will for them to be guided to faith such that they would be compelled to accept, thereby nullifying their choice. This is what the apparent context of the noble verse requires.

However, in similar passages of His speech, He, the Glorified, did not base His lack of such a will solely on the necessity of compulsion. For instance, His saying: **وَلَوْ شِئْنا لَآتَيْنا كُلَّ نَفْسٍ هُداها ، وَلكِنْ حَقَّ الْقَوْلُ مِنِّي لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ** — **"Had We wished, We would have given every soul its guidance, but My word has become due: ‘I will surely fill hell with jinn and humans, all together’"** (As-Sajdah: 13). He refers there to something like His saying: **قالَ فَالْحَقُّ وَالْحَقَّ أَقُولُ لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنْكَ وَمِمَّنْ تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ** — **"He said, ‘This is the truth, and the truth I say: I will surely fill hell with you and with those of them who follow you, all together’"** (Sad: 84-85). Thus, He explained that the non-realization of His will to guide them all is due to His decree, which He decreed in response to Iblis's oath that he would surely lead them all astray except for His devoted servants among them.

In another place, He attributed the decree to their own deviance (غوايتهم). He said in the story of Adam and Iblis: **قالَ رَبِّ بِما أَغْوَيْتَنِي لَأُزَيِّنَنَّ لَهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَأُغْوِيَنَّهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ ، إِلَّا عِبادَكَ مِنْهُمُ الْمُخْلَصِينَ قالَ هذا صِراطٌ عَلَيَّ مُسْتَقِيمٌ ، إِنَّ عِبادِي لَيْسَ لَكَ عَلَيْهِمْ سُلْطانٌ إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْغاوِينَ ، وَإِنَّ جَهَنَّمَ لَمَوْعِدُهُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ** — **"He said, ‘My Lord! As You have consigned me to perversity, I will surely glamorize [evil] for them on the earth, and I will surely pervert them all, except Your exclusive servants among them.’ He said, ‘This is a straight path [leading] to Me. Indeed, as for My servants, you shall have no authority over them, except such of the perverse as follow you. Indeed hell is the tryst of them all’"** (Al-Hijr: 39-43). Iblis also attributed this to them in what Allah narrated of his speech to them on the Day of Resurrection: **وَقالَ الشَّيْطانُ لَمَّا قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ إِنَّ اللهَ وَعدَكُمْ وَعْدَ الْحَقِّ وَوَعَدْتُكُمْ فَأَخْلَفْتُكُمْ** — **"When the matter is decided, Satan will say, ‘Indeed Allah made you a true promise. I [too] made you promises, but I failed you...’"** until he said: **إِنِّي كَفَرْتُ بِما أَشْرَكْتُمُونِ مِنْ قَبْلُ** — **"I disavow your having staggered me [with Him] before"** (Ibrahim: 22).

These verses explain that sins, including polytheism (الشرك), end in human deviance (غواية), and deviance ends in the human soul itself. This does not contradict what appears in other verses—that man cannot will unless Allah wills the "willing" from him—such as His saying: **إِنَّ هذِهِ تَذْكِرَةٌ فَمَنْ شاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلى رَبِّهِ سَبِيلاً ، وَما تَشاؤُنَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ** — **"This is indeed a reminder. So let anyone who wishes take the way to his Lord. But you do not wish unless Allah wishes"** (Al-Insan: 29-30), and His saying: **إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا ذِكْرٌ لِلْعالَمِينَ ، لِمَنْ شاءَ مِنْكُمْ أَنْ يَسْتَقِيمَ ، وَما تَشاؤُنَ إِلَّا أَنْ يَشاءَ اللهُ رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ** — **"It is just a reminder for all the nations, for those of you who wish to walk straight. But you do not wish unless it is wished by Allah, the Lord of all the worlds"** (At-Takwir: 27-29).

Thus, although the realization of man's will depends on the will of Allah, the Glorified, Allah does not will the "willing" from him unless he is prepared for it by the goodness of his inner self (سريرته) and exposes himself to His mercy. He, the Exalted, said: **وَيَهْدِي إِلَيْهِ مَنْ أَنابَ** — **"and He guides to Himself those who turn [to Him] penitently"** (Ar-Ra'd: 27), meaning those who incline and return to Him. As for the transgressor (الفاسق) whose heart has swerved, who clings to the earth and inclines toward deviance, Allah does not will his guidance nor does He cover him with His mercy, as He said: **يُضِلُّ بِهِ كَثِيراً وَيَهْدِي بِهِ كَثِيراً وَما يُضِلُّ بِهِ إِلَّا الْفاسِقِينَ** — **"He misleads many thereby, and He guides many thereby; and He misleads no one thereby except the transgressors"** (Al-Baqarah: 26), and He said: **فَلَمَّا زاغُوا أَزاغَ اللهُ قُلُوبَهُمْ** — **"So when they swerved, Allah made their hearts swerve"** (As-Saff: 5), and He said: **وَلَوْ شِئْنا لَرَفَعْناهُ بِها وَلكِنَّهُ أَخْلَدَ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ وَاتَّبَعَ هَواهُ** — **"Had We wished, We would have surely exalted him by their means, but he clung to the earth and followed his low desires"** (Al-A'raf: 176).

In short, the religious mission only follows the path of choice (الاختيار), and Divine signs are only revealed with due regard for choice. Allah, the Glorified, only guides to Himself those who expose themselves to His mercy and prepare themselves for His guidance through the path of choice.

By this, another somewhat difficult problem is resolved: We concede that His sending a sign that compels them to believe and forces them to accept the religious mission contradicts the foundation of choice upon which the structure of the religious mission is built. However, why is it not permissible for Allah to will the faith of all people just as He willed the faith of those who believed—by willing that all of them "will" [to believe], just as He willed specifically from the believers that they "will"—and then reveal a sign that drives them toward guidance and clothes them in faith without thereby nullifying their choice and freedom of action?

The answer is that while this is possible in itself, it contradicts the universal law (الناموس العام) in the world of causes (عالم الأسباب) and the system of preparation and emanation (نظام الاستعداد والإفاضة). Guidance is only emanated upon those who are God-conscious (اتقى الله) and purify their souls—"He who purifies it is indeed felicitous"—and error only afflicts those who turn away from the remembrance of their Lord and stunt their souls—"and he who stunts it is indeed a failure." His being afflicted by error means that the human being is denied guidance. He, the Exalted, said: **مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ الْعاجِلَةَ عَجَّلْنا لَهُ فِيها ما نَشاءُ لِمَنْ نُرِيدُ ثُمَّ جَعَلْنا لَهُ جَهَنَّمَ يَصْلاها مَذْمُوماً مَدْحُوراً ، وَمَنْ أَرادَ الْآخِرَةَ وَسَعى لَها سَعْيَها وَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ فَأُولئِكَ كانَ سَعْيُهُمْ مَشْكُوراً ، كُلًّا نُمِدُّ هؤُلاءِ وَهَؤُلاءِ مِنْ عَطاءِ رَبِّكَ وَما كانَ عَطاءُ رَبِّكَ مَحْظُوراً** — **"Whoever desires this transitory life, We expedite for him therein whatever We wish, for whomever We desire. Then We appoint hell for him, to enter it, blamed and spurned. Whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it with an effort worthy of it, should he be faithful—the endeavour of such will be well-appreciated. To these and to those—to all We extend the bounty of your Lord, and the bounty of your Lord is not barred"** (Al-Isra: 18-20), meaning it is not withheld. Thus, Allah, the Glorified, extends to every soul from His bounty that which it deserves; if it desires good, it is given it, and if it desires evil, it is given it—meaning it is barred from good. Had Allah willed for every soul, whether righteous or wicked, to "will" good and devote itself to faith and godwariness through the path of choice, that would have entailed the nullification of the universal system and the corruption of the order of causes.

This is supported by the following verse, namely His saying: **إِنَّما يَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ يَسْمَعُونَ** — **"Only those who listen will respond"** until the end of the verse, according to the meaning that will follow.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **إِنَّما يَسْتَجِيبُ الَّذِينَ يَسْمَعُونَ وَالْمَوْتى يَبْعَثُهُمُ اللهُ ثُمَّ إِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُونَ** — **"Only those who listen will respond. As for the dead, Allah will resurrect them, then to Him they will be returned."** The verse is like an explanation for His saying: **وَإِنْ كانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ إِعْراضُهُمْ** — **"And if their aversion is hard on you"** until the end of the verse. Its summary is that you cannot turn them away from this aversion, nor obtain a sign that drives them to faith. He explained in this verse that they are like the dead (الموتى); they have no consciousness nor hearing such that they might perceive the meaning of the religious mission and hear the call of the caller, who is the Prophet (ص).

These visible human forms are of two types: one type is alive and hears—and indeed, only those who hear respond—and the other type is dead and does not hear, even if they appear in the form of the living. Their hearing of the speech depends on Allah resurrecting them, and He will surely resurrect them, and they will hear what they were unable to hear in the world, as Allah narrated from them in His saying: **وَلَوْ تَرى إِذِ الْمُجْرِمُونَ ناكِسُوا رُؤُسِهِمْ عندَ رَبِّهِمْ رَبَّنا أَبْصَرْنا وَسَمِعْنا فَارْجِعْنا نَعْمَلْ صالِحاً إِنَّا مُوقِنُونَ** — **"Were you to see when the guilty hang their heads before their Lord [confessing], ‘Our Lord! We have seen and heard; so send us back [to the world] that we may act righteously. Indeed [now] we have certainty’"** (As-Sajdah: 12).

The speech is delivered by way of metonymy (الكناية). The intended meaning of "those who listen" (الذين يسمعون) is the believers, and by "the dead" (الموتى) is meant those among the polytheists and others who turn away from responding to the call. The description of believers as having life (الحياة) and hearing (السمع), and the description of disbelievers as having death (الموت) and deafness (الصمم), is repeated in His speech, the Exalted, as He said: **أَوَمَنْ كانَ مَيْتاً فَأَحْيَيْناهُ وَجَعَلْنا لَهُ نُوراً يَمْشِي بِهِ فِي النَّاسِ كَمَنْ مَثَلُهُ فِي الظُّلُماتِ لَيْسَ بِخارِجٍ مِنْها** — **"Is he who was lifeless, then We gave him life and provided him with a light by which he walks among the people, like one who is in a manifold darkness from which he cannot emerge?"** (Al-An'am: 122), and He, the Exalted, said: **إِنَّكَ لا تُسْمِعُ الْمَوْتى وَلا تُسْمِعُ الصُّمَّ الدُّعاءَ إِذا وَلَّوْا مُدْبِرِينَ ، وَما أَنْتَ بِهادِي الْعُمْيِ عَنْ ضَلالَتِهِمْ إِنْ تُسْمِعُ إِلَّا مَنْ يُؤْمِنُ بِآياتِنا فَهُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ** — **"You cannot make the dead hear, nor can you make the deaf hear the call when they turn their backs [and] flee. Nor can you lead the blind out of their error. You can make only those hear who believe in Our signs and have submitted"** (An-Naml: 80-81), among many other verses.

In some previous discussions, another meaning for these attributes—which the majority of commentators take as metonymy and metaphor—has been repeated, suggesting they possess a literal (الحقيقة) sense; so let it be referred to.

In the verse, there is an indication that Allah will make the disbelievers and polytheists understand the truth and hear His call in the Hereafter, just as He made the believers understand and hear in this world. Thus, man, whether a believer or a disbeliever, has no escape from understanding the truth, sooner or later.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *Tafsir al-Qummi*, it is stated: In the narration of Abu al-Jarud from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him), he said: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) used to love that al-Harith ibn 'Amir ibn Nawfal ibn 'Abd Manaf would embrace Islam. The Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله) called him and strove with him to submit, but wretchedness (الشقاء) overcame him. This was hard on the Messenger of Allah (صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله), so Allah revealed: **وَإِنْ كانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكَ إِعْراضُهُمْ** — **'And if their aversion is hard on you'** until His saying **نَفَقاً فِي الْأَرْضِ** — **'a tunnel into the earth,'** meaning a burrow (سربا)."

**I say:** The narration, despite its weakness and interrupted chain (إرسال), does not align with the outward sense of the numerous narrations indicating that the Surah was revealed all at once. However, it is possible to reconcile it by suggesting that the cause (السبب) occurred before the revelation of the Surah, and then the verse alluded to the realized cause by way of application (الانطباق).
